Hi Logan —

Quick note ahead of next week's workshop: our execs (10 total) are flying in on the 15th, arriving between 3:30–5:15pm. A few asks from our side:

- Can Redwood provide a dedicated arrival lounge / staging area where riders can wait? Ideally heated indoor space near the loading zone.
- Two execs bring personal security with separate cars (driver + minivan). We need clear curbside staging and a driver check-in point.
- Dietary heads-up: 2 vegetarian, 1 pescatarian, 1 nut allergy.

Appreciate it if you can confirm a point person for drivers and kitchen handoff. Also: we expect some last-minute schedule changes; is there a single phone line for on-day ops?

Thanks —

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for the conversation last week. Based on our kickoff call and your usage profile, I sketched three renewal structures that try to balance predictability for StellarNet and flexibility as your usage grows:

1) Annualized multi-year (3yr) with step-up commit: predictable lower unit price with pre-negotiated annual increases tied to committed throughput. Includes a 10% burst buffer each year.

2) Convertible credit ladder (proposal attached): an initial committed bucket plus a ladder of progressive credits that convert into prepaid capacity if usage exceeds thresholds. Credits act as both discounting and a short runway ...

Hi Hannah,

Quick ask — we're hitting a hard stop on our BrightCart pilot tonight at midnight PT. The team is about to exercise a couple more high-traffic flows and we expect another ~40k tokens of usage, but our trial quota will cut the test off.

Can Redwood grant a one-time 48-hour top-up or token credit so we can complete the last two scenarios? It would let us finish a spiky run that accurately shows latency behavior under load. If there are any sign-offs needed from your side we can take a short form — we just need the extension today.

Appreciate a fast reply.

— Samir
